paper_title: Analysis of Interference Patterns in Molecular Ionization

methods.description: This method investigates how interference patterns in molecular ionization are influenced by the orientation of the molecule with respect to the laser-polarization direction. The analysis compares the ATI spectrum for different molecular orientations and examines the effects of internuclear distance on the interference patterns.

steps.description: Investigate how the interference patterns are influenced by the orientation of the molecule with respect to the laser-polarization direction.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze interference patterns based on molecular orientation and laser polarization

steps.description: Compare the entire ATI spectrum consisting of the direct and the rescattered electrons in the atomic and the molecular case.
steps.searchable_summary: compare ATI spectra for atomic and molecular cases

steps.description: Analyze the dependence of the spectra on the laser-field polarization in the plateau energy region.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze spectra dependence on laser-field polarization

steps.description: Investigate the individual contributions to the transition matrix element.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze contributions to transition matrix element

steps.description: Present density plots for a complete picture of the angle-resolved ATI spectrum.
steps.searchable_summary: create density plots for angle-resolved ATI spectrum

steps.description: Analyze the angle-resolved spectra for different internuclear separations.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze angle-resolved spectra for internuclear separations

steps.description: Determine the function R(δE) from the analytical formula for the classical cutoff energy E(θ).
steps.searchable_summary: model function R(δE) using classical cutoff energy formula
